title: thymus serpyllum subsp. chamaedrys
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. chamaedrys, also known as wild thyme, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of wild thyme include its ability to soothe coughs, relieve digestive issues, and boost the immune system, providing relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Wild thyme can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, allowing for its benefits to be utilized in a range of applications.
